Field.Name: Name
Field.DefenceHint: Flat damage reduction. Incoming damage is reduced by this amount (minimum 0) before HP loss.
Field.MagicalHint: Flag this item as magical. Magical items are excluded from Tinkerer repair, Quick Craft, and Fix Construct flows.
Field.AdornmentHint: Flag this item as an adornment (ring, amulet, sigil, talisman, charm, or similar). Only flagged items can occupy the two adornment inventory slots.
Field.PortraitHint: Click to choose an image for this character (opens Tokenizer if installed; hold Shift for the image browser).

Hint.CombosTriggerSkills: Combinations may trigger class skills, Legendary Weapon effects, or instrument abilities.
Hint.EffectTable: d10 lookup table — fill the entries you want this instrument to roll on. Empty entries roll as “no effect”.
Hint.EffectTableBanded: The green band (1-4) rolls on Success — reserve it for effects that help the party. The red band (5-10) rolls on Fail or Fumble — reserve it for chaos and randomness. Range rows fill both indices with the same text.
Hint.Tagline: Optional italicised phrase shown under the instrument name (e.g. “The obvious choice for the obvious bard”).
Hint.Level: Mark all in one row to advance a level
Hint.CombatTalents: Choose one combat talent per level. Write it down here.
Hint.Spellbook: Spells live below; the spellbook list is just for tracking which are inscribed.
Hint.InstrumentSlots: Drop an instrument here. Slot 2 unlocks at level 3, slot 3 at level 5. Worn — quick to ready.
Hint.DragDarkSpells: Drag dark spells from the FLAIL Dark Spells compendium onto this list to learn them.
Hint.SpecialtyTags: Comma-separated. -1 TH if NONE of these match the wielder's class weapon specialty list. Use specific names (shortSword) and broad ones (blade) so any rule wording can match.

Placeholder.Tagline: e.g. “The obvious choice for the obvious bard”

Class.SigilWorn: Sigil worn — tokens can be spent
Class.SigilMissing: Sigil not worn — tokens cannot be spent

Bard.WeaponSpec: crossbow, dagger, short bow, short sword, and similar.
Bard.Gear: grub, lantern, short sword, instrument, 4d6 coins.

BoneWhisperer.WeaponSpec: dagger, flail, scythe, short sword, and similar.
BoneWhisperer.MainItem: spirit lantern (always lit if spirit is not depleted).
BoneWhisperer.Gear: grub, spirit lantern, random scroll, weapon, 3d6 coins.

Cleric.Gear: grub, torches, holy symbol, weapon, d6 coins.

Cutthroat.WeaponSpec: blowgun, crossbow, dagger, short sword, and other small weapons.
Cutthroat.Gear: grub, thieves' tools, guild sigil, two weapons, 4d10 coins.

Druid.WeaponSpec: axe, bows, club, mace, quarterstaff, sling, spear.
Druid.Gear: grub, torches, weapon, herb, random animal companion, d6 coins.

Tinkerer.WeaponSpec: bows, blowgun, dagger, hand axe, short sword, and similar.
Tinkerer.Gear: grub, lantern, weapon, basic armour, sparkle of life, gadget belt, 6d6 coins.

Warrior.Gear: grub, torches, weapon, basic armour, helmet, 3d6 coins.

Wizard.WeaponSpec: crossbow, dagger, quarterstaff, short sword, wand, and similar.
Wizard.Gear: grub, torches, spellbook, weapon, random essence (see potions), 2d10 coins.

Inventory.AdornmentPlaceholder: ring, amulet, sigil, talisman, or charm

Hit.DeathBlowDesc: Instantly slain — or roll on the Death Table if a PC.
Hit.FumbleDesc: Hit an ally for d8 OR mark usage on a carried item.

WitnessMe.Name: Witness Me
WitnessMe.AllyBuff: All allies gain +1 die to hit on their next attack.

Action.MarkUsageHint: Mark one usage dot on the weapon used.
Action.ApplyDamageHint: Apply this damage to all selected tokens.
Action.NegateWithArmourHint: Mark worn armour to negate 1s from this attack.

Armour.NegatePrompt: You've been hit — the roll includes {ones} ones. Mark worn armour to negate.
Armour.NegateHint: Each mark on a piece of armour negates one 1. You may mark the same or multiple pieces any number of times.
Armour.NegateVerdictMiss: Attack fully negated — no damage taken.
Armour.NegateVerdictDeath: Attack still lands as a Death Blow.
Armour.NegateWasted: {count} mark(s) wasted — nothing else to negate.
Armour.ProficiencyWarningHeadline: No Armour Proficiency — Disadvantage on all physical tasks.

Roll.InstrumentUsedOutHint: This instrument was played to a fail or fumble this scene. Rest, or click the reset button, to play again.
Roll.InstrumentResetHint: New combat or social situation — click to allow this instrument to be played again.

ModifierDialog.Title: Modify To Hit — {weapon}
ModifierDialog.Prompt: Adjust the number of dice for this attack with {weapon}.
ModifierDialog.Hint: Positive values add extra dice to the To Hit pool. Negative values remove dice from the pool. The pool cannot drop below 1 die. Range: -3 to +3.

Combat.EndReminder: Roll usage for all weapons used.

Construct.AttackingHint: Constructs can only wield weapons whose TH is equal or below their own TH value. DMG is either the weapon's or the construct's DMG score — whichever is higher.
Construct.InherentAttackHint: Roll the construct's inherent attack — uses its own TH as the dice pool and its DMG per die. Shift for advantage, Ctrl for disadvantage.
Construct.SavesRollHint: Click to roll a d20 Saves check.
Construct.ImprovementHint: 5 improvement points per level, from level 1 onward. Spend on improvements — maximum 2 points on the same improvement per level. Carried inventory slots unlock first, then stashed.
Construct.SparkleActive: Sparkle of Life equipped — construct active
Construct.SparkleMissing: Sparkle of Life not equipped — construct inert
Construct.UnlockCarriedHint: Spend an improvement point to unlock one more carried slot.
Construct.UnlockStashedHint: Spend an improvement point to unlock one more stashed slot. Only available once all carried slots are unlocked.

Improvement.LockHint: Click to lock the Improvements tab and prevent further changes.
Improvement.UnlockHint: Click to unlock the Improvements tab so improvements can be added or removed.
Improvement.PointsSunk: of which {sunk} sunk in broken improvements

Construct.NeedsBreakDownBanner: HP reduced to 0 — this construct needs to be broken down.
Construct.BreakDownTitle: Break Down — Choose an improvement to lose
Construct.BreakDownPrompt: {name} has been reduced to 0 HP. The construct is broken. Choose one improvement to lose.
Construct.BreakDownHint: The chosen improvement is refunded from the ledger but its effect is reversed — you may re-buy it later if you have unspent points.
Construct.BrokenNoImprovements: No improvements were on the sheet — nothing to lose beyond the break itself.
Construct.BrokenRebuildHint: Rebuild will take {days} days and {coins} coins.
Construct.RebuildReadyHint: Clicking Rebuild will deduct {coins} coins from the linked Tinkerer and restore the construct to full HP.
Construct.RebuildBlockedNoOwner: No linked Tinkerer. Set an owner on the Sketch card first.
Construct.RebuildBlockedNoCoins: The linked Tinkerer needs {needed} coins to rebuild (currently has {have}).
Construct.RebuildBody: After {days} days of work, {owner} rebuilds {name}. {coins} coins spent. HP restored to {hp}/{hp}.
Construct.ReassembleConfirm: Starting a Reassemble session on {name} will PERMANENTLY drop 1 improvement point from the pool. While in reassemble mode, each improvement removed costs 100 coins from {owner} ({coins} coins on hand). Adding improvements is free (uses freed points as normal). Continue?
Construct.ReassembleStartHint: Begin a reassemble session — drops 1 improvement point permanently; each removal within the session costs 100 coins.
Construct.ReassembleModeHint: Each improvement removed while in this mode costs 100 coins from the linked Tinkerer. Adding improvements uses freed points as normal.
Construct.ReassembleBlockedLocked: The Improvements tab must be unlocked before reassembling.
Construct.ReassembleBlockedBroken: This construct is broken — rebuild it before reassembling.
Construct.ReassembleBlockedNoOwner: No linked Tinkerer. Set an owner on the Sketch card first.
Construct.ReassembleCardBody: {owner} begins reshaping the construct. 1 improvement point is permanently sunk. Reassemble mode is now active — each removal in the tab costs 100 coins.

Tinkerer.ResourcefulnessDescription: Post-combat usage rolls trigger on 5-6 (instead of 4-6). Non-magical items can be repaired by cannibalising materials from another item.
Tinkerer.ResourcefulnessTooltip: Tinkerer's Resourcefulness raises the usage threshold to 5-6.
Tinkerer.RepairPrompt: Pick a worn item to repair and a donor to sacrifice materials from. On a successful DEX save, the target is repaired and one usage is marked on the donor.
Tinkerer.RepairHint: Donor should share similar materials with the target — GM judgement. Magical items cannot be repaired or donate.
Tinkerer.RepairFailBody: The repair on {target} fails. No materials expended.
Tinkerer.QuickCraftPrompt: Describe the invention and pick 3-6 items to discard as components. Then roll DEX. Items are consumed regardless of the roll's outcome.
Tinkerer.QuickCraftInventionPlaceholder: e.g. A tripwire trap that drops a net; a smoke bomb; a spring-loaded dart launcher…
Tinkerer.QuickCraftFailBody: Fail. The contraption fizzles — components wasted, no effect.
Tinkerer.QuickCraftFumbleBody: Fumble. The invention backfires on the party — GM adjudicates the consequences.
Tinkerer.FixConstructPrompt: Fix {name}? Currently at {cur}/{max} HP. Choose one method.
Tinkerer.FixConstructMarkHeading: Option A — Spend a turn, mark 2 items
Tinkerer.FixConstructMarkHint: Mark usage on two non-magical items (from either sheet), then roll a DEX save. Success → +2 HP. Fail → items are still marked, but no HP repaired.
Tinkerer.FixConstructMarkBlocked: You need at least two items with usage headroom to use this method.
Tinkerer.FixConstructMarkFail: The save failed. Materials were consumed but no HP was restored.
Tinkerer.FixConstructDiscardHint: Discard one non-magical item to instantly restore +4 HP. No save required.
Tinkerer.FixConstructDiscardBlocked: No non-magical items available to discard.
Tinkerer.FixConstructPickPrompt: You own more than one construct. Which one are you fixing?
Tinkerer.GadgetRecoveryClean: Triplet on a hit, but {belt} has no usage marks to remove.
Tinkerer.GadgetRecoveryMissing: Triplet on a hit, but no Gadget Belt is on this sheet.

Warrior.IronFistHint: Iron Fist — bare-handed attack from the Martial Artist Basic talent. Shift = +1 die, Ctrl = -1 die, Alt = modifier dialog.
Warrior.FeatCheckboxHint: Tick to route this attack through the Extraordinary Feat mechanic — attack + STR/DEX save (auto-detected from weapon type), with the outcome verdict posted to chat.
Warrior.FeatButtonHint: Extraordinary Feat — declare a narrative consequence, then roll attack + STR/DEX save. Shift = save advantage, Ctrl = save disadvantage.
Warrior.FeatDamageApply: The feat works. Apply damage from the attack card above and resolve the narrative consequence.
Warrior.FeatDamageNoApply: The feat backfires. The attack fails entirely — do NOT apply damage from the attack card, even if the attack roll would have hit normally.
Warrior.FeatFumbleGuidance: Standard attack fumble effects still apply (as shown on the attack card). On top of that, the GM narrates an additional consequence tied to the failed feat.

FeatVerdict.success: Feat works! Attack hits with narrative consequence.
FeatVerdict.fumble: FUMBLE — standard treatment plus GM-narrated consequence.

Wizard.SpellbookEmptyHint: Drag spells from the Wizard Spells compendium here to build your Master Spellbook.
Wizard.MasterEmptyHint: Pick a Master to unlock their spell repertoire. Common (arcane) spells work regardless.
Wizard.MasterDragHint: Choose two of these spells and drag them from the Wizard Spells compendium into your spellbook.
Wizard.MasterLockHint: Lock in this Master apprenticeship. Only the GM can unlock afterwards.
Wizard.MasterLockedGmOnly: The Master apprenticeship is locked. Only the GM can unlock it.
Wizard.ManaHint: Current mana out of the maximum (INT + level). Restored to full on a long rest.
Wizard.CastButtonHint: Cast this spell — pick how much mana to spend.
Wizard.CastDialogWarning: Rolling one 6 = Drained · two 6s = Side Effect · three 6s = Glorious Death.
Wizard.CastRiskDrainedText: The wizard gains the Drained condition after casting.
Wizard.CastAddDrainedHint: Drag the Drained condition from your compendium into your inventory to apply it.
Wizard.CastRiskSideEffectMissing: Side effect table entry missing — GM narrates.
Wizard.CastRiskDeathText: The spell resolves as described. The wizard gloriously dies afterwards.
Wizard.ReadMagicHint: INT save to learn a spell from an arcane source or cast from a scroll. Shift = advantage, Ctrl = disadvantage.

Instrument.CritPrompt: CRIT! Choose any result from the Instrument Table.
Instrument.UsedOutBanner: This instrument cannot be played again in the same combat or social situation.

Success.Title: Success band (1-4)
Success.Sub: Rolled on a successful CHA save. Good for the party.

Chaos.Title: Chaos band (5-10)
Chaos.Sub: Rolled on a Fail (d10) or Fumble (d6+4). Chaos and randomness.

Empty.NoLoose: No loose items. Drag items here from the inventory grid.

Hint.CombosTriggerSkills: Combinations may trigger class skills, Legendary Weapon effects, or instrument abilities.
Hint.EffectTable: d10 lookup table — fill the entries you want this instrument to roll on. Empty entries roll as “no effect”.

NPC.AttacksPerRoundHint: This creature makes more than one attack per round — resolve each in turn.

Dialog.DeleteItemBody: Permanently delete {name}? This cannot be undone.

Notify.SelectTokens: Select at least one token to apply damage.
Notify.ZoneRejectsMissingFlag: {zone} slots only accept items flagged as an adornment. Tick the Adornment checkbox on the {item} item sheet first.
Notify.MultiSlotNoRoom: {item} takes more than one slot — not enough room at that position in the {zone} column.
Notify.MultiSlotBlocked: {item} would extend into a slot occupied by {blocker}.
Notify.MultiSlotBlockedLocked: {item} would extend into a locked slot.
Notify.TwoHandedNeedsFreeHand: {weapon} needs both hands to fire — {blocker} is in the way.
Notify.NegateArmourNotOwner: You don't own any of the character targets on this attack.
Notify.NoArmourToMark: No worn armour with unused usage dots — nothing to mark.
Notify.NotADarkSpell: This panel only accepts dark spells.
Notify.NotAWizardSpell: This panel only accepts wizard spells (arcane, flame, shadow, ooze, or illusion).
Notify.NotAPrimalGift: This panel only accepts Primal Gift items.
Notify.NotAGadget: This panel only accepts Gadget items.
Notify.NotADamageGadget: This action only fires damage-category gadgets.
Notify.GadgetAlreadyUsed: {name} has already been released today. Reset on long rest.
Notify.GadgetNoMechanic: {name} has no mechanical rules wired — GM narrates the effect.
Notify.JOATBardOnly: Jack of All Trades only applies to Bard characters.
Notify.NotAJoatItem: Jack of All Trades only accepts talents, gadgets, or wizard spells.
Notify.JoatSpellAlreadyUsed: {name} has already been cast today. Reset on long rest.
Notify.NotAThievingTalent: This panel only accepts Thieving Talent items.
Notify.InstrumentNotCarried: {name} must be carried (in hands or on the body) to play. Move it from the satchel first.
Notify.InstrumentUsedOut: {name} was played to a fail or fumble. Rest, or click reset, before playing it again in this scene.
Notify.NotAPrayer: This panel only accepts divine prayers.
Notify.NoHolySymbol: You need to be carrying or wearing your holy symbol to cast a prayer.
Notify.LayOnHandsNoTarget: Target a token first — Lay on Hands needs a recipient.
Notify.LayOnHandsMultipleTargets: Multiple tokens targeted; only the first will receive Lay on Hands.
Notify.HolyRayNoTarget: Target a token first — Holy Ray needs a recipient.
Notify.Incapacitated: Petrified — you cannot move or take actions.
Notify.NoSigil: Your guild sigil must be worn (in hands or body slot) to spend tokens.
Notify.NotAGuild: This panel only accepts guild items.
Notify.NoGuild: Drag a guild onto your sheet first.
Notify.OpportunisticStrikeWeaponGone: The original weapon is no longer on the sheet — can't fire the follow-up attack.
Notify.GiftNotSelected: This primal gift isn't currently selected.
Notify.ShapeshiftAlreadyActive: Already shifted — revert to human first.
Notify.ShapeshiftNoGifts: The Druid has no primal gifts selected — nothing to shift into.
Notify.WeaponLockedInBeastForm: You can't use that weapon while in beast form — use one of the beast's natural attacks.
Notify.NothingToRepair: No worn items on the sheet to repair.
Notify.NoDonorAvailable: No item on the sheet has spare capacity to donate materials.
Notify.RepairSameItem: The target and donor must be different items.
Notify.QuickCraftNotEnoughItems: Need at least 3 physical items on the sheet to Quick Craft.
Notify.QuickCraftBadCount: Quick Craft requires 3 to 6 components — no more, no fewer.
Notify.QuickCraftNoInvention: Describe what you're building before rolling.
Notify.ConstructWeaponTooHeavy: This weapon's TH exceeds the construct's own TH — it can't wield it.
Notify.ConstructNoInherentAttack: This construct has TH 0 — its inherent attack can't be rolled until TH is improved.
Notify.ConstructSlotOccupied: That slot is already occupied. Move or delete the current item first.
Notify.ConstructCarriedFirst: Unlock all carried slots before spending on stashed slots.
Notify.ImprovementLocked: Unlock the Improvements tab before making changes.
Notify.ImprovementNotEnoughPoints: Not enough construct points to buy that improvement.
Notify.ImprovementMaxPerLevel: You can only buy the same improvement twice per level.
Notify.ImprovementAtMax: That improvement is already at its cap.
Notify.ImprovementNoInstance: The construct doesn't have that improvement to remove.
Notify.ImprovementAlreadyOwned: The construct already has that ability.
Notify.FixConstructNoLink: No construct is linked to this Tinkerer. Set the owner on the construct's sheet first.
Notify.FixConstructFullHp: {name} is at full HP — no repair needed.
Notify.FixConstructNoItems: No non-magical items are available on either sheet to spend.
Notify.FixConstructSameItem: The two marked items must be different.
Notify.FixConstructBroken: {name} is broken — repair with Rebuild, not Fix Construct.
Notify.ConstructBroken: This construct is broken — no actions until it's rebuilt.
Notify.RebuildNoOwner: No linked Tinkerer. Set an owner on the construct's Sketch card first.
Notify.ReassembleLocked: Unlock the Improvements tab before starting a reassemble session.
Notify.ReassembleBroken: The construct is broken — rebuild it before reassembling.
Notify.ReassembleNoOwner: No linked Tinkerer. Set an owner on the construct's Sketch card first.
Notify.ReassembleNoCoins: {owner} needs 100 coins to remove an improvement in reassemble mode (currently has {have}).
Notify.FeatWarriorOnly: Extraordinary Feat is a Warrior class ability.
Notify.IronFistWarriorOnly: Iron Fist is a Warrior class ability.
Notify.IronFistRequiresTalent: Iron Fist requires the Martial Artist Basic combat talent.
Notify.WizardMasterUnset: Choose a Master Apprenticeship before adding master-specific spells. Common (arcane) spells are always accepted.
Notify.WizardMasterMismatch: {spell} is from another Master's repertoire — this wizard is apprenticed under {master}.
Notify.WizardMasterUnlockGmOnly: Only the GM can unlock the Master Apprenticeship.
Notify.NoManaAvailable: No mana left — take a long rest to refill.
Notify.ReadMagicWizardOnly: Read Magic is a Wizard class ability.
Notify.MiracleCallSpent: Miracle Call has already been used this session. Click Reset to start over.
Notify.PickReligionFirst: Pick a religion before dragging prayers onto your sheet.
Notify.PrayerWrongReligion: {prayer} is a {prayerReligion} prayer; this character follows {actorReligion}.
Notify.AlreadyKnown: {name} is already in this character's known spells.
Notify.NoSpiritToCast: Need at least 1 spirit (and 1 INT) to cast a dark spell.

Dialog.Title: Cast Dark Spell — {spell}
Dialog.Warning: Each natural 6 brings ruin: 1 → Drained · 2 → Side Effect · 3 → retire.

Drained.Title: Drained!
Drained.Body: One natural 6 — the caster gains a Drained condition (-1 INT). Apply via Conditions card.

Retire.Title: Retire
Retire.Body: Three natural 6s — the caster becomes an undead monster and is retired. The GM resolves narrative consequences.

AnimateDead.Label: Animate Dead
AnimateDead.Hint: Spend spirit equal to the target level. Make an INT save to revive the target. On success, the target is raised as an undead ally for a number of watches equal to your level. On a fail, the target is raised, but acts on its own accord.
AnimateDead.Tooltip: Roll INT save (Shift = advantage, Ctrl = disadvantage)

Religion.SelectPrompt: Select a religion above to see your god, holy symbol, weapons, armour, and Lay on Hands fumble.
Religion.NoPrayersUntilChosen: Your divine prayers appear here once you choose a religion.

Fail.Title: Falters
Fail.Body: The prayer fails to take effect; the chance of fumbling rises by 1.

LayOnHands.Tooltip: Target a token, then make a LUCK save to heal them (d6 + level).
LayOnHands.Title: Lay on Hands

Fail.Title: No Effect
Fail.Body: The touch fails to channel divine healing; fumble range rises by 1.

MiracleCall.Tooltip: Once per session. Speak a wish aloud, roll d6 per word, each 6 triggers God's Wrath.
MiracleCall.ResetTooltip: Reset Miracle Call (use at start of a new session).
MiracleCall.Title: Miracle Call
MiracleCall.OncePerSession: Once per session — reset on the sheet at session start.

Granted.Title: Granted
Granted.Body: The deity answers — the wish takes effect. The GM resolves the miracle narratively.

Dialog.Title: Miracle Call
Dialog.Prompt: Speak your wish aloud — thematically appropriate for your deity. Each spoken word rolls a d6; each natural 6 triggers a God's Wrath.
Dialog.Warning: The miracle always occurs. The risk is in the words.

CelestialAid.Title: Celestial Aid
CelestialAid.Trigger: Full House on a To Hit roll!
CelestialAid.Prompt: Your god appears, bestowing divine assistance. Choose one:

HolyRay.Title: Holy Ray
HolyRay.Picked: Holy Ray chosen — d20 damage to one target.

FullBlessSelf.Picked: Full Bless chosen — Cleric healed to full.

FullBlessAllies.Picked: Full Bless chosen — all allies healed to full.

LitanyOfClarity.Title: Litany of Clarity
LitanyOfClarity.Picked: Litany of Clarity chosen — fumble range reset to 20.

Talents.Hint: Cutthroats start with five talents (from their guild), gaining one more at each level. Add a talent to unlock click-to-roll its save with advantage.
Talents.RollHint: Click to roll a {save} save with advantage.
Talents.NoneSelected: No talents yet. Drag talents from the FLAIL Cutthroat Thieving Talents compendium onto this panel.
Talents.AllSelected: All twelve talents have been added.
Talents.DropHint: Drag more talents from the FLAIL Cutthroat Thieving Talents compendium to add them. Advantage on the matching save when the action is attempted.

GuildTokens.SpendTooltip: Spend one guild token. Requires the sigil to be worn.
GuildTokens.ResetTooltip: Reset tokens to level (at session start).
GuildTokens.Recouped: Mark of the Guild — token recouped!
GuildTokens.RecoupFull: Mark of the Guild — already at maximum tokens

Guild.DropPrompt: Drag a guild here to set your affiliation.
Guild.RemoveConfirm: Remove {name}? You'll lose access to the guild's special actions until a new guild is dragged in.

PrimalGift.DropHintFilled: Drag more Primal Gifts from the compendium to add them. Each gift is drag-and-drop from the FLAIL Druid Primal Gifts pack.
PrimalGift.DropHintEmpty: Drag Primal Gifts from the FLAIL Druid Primal Gifts compendium onto this panel to acquire them.
PrimalGift.DurationNote: Note: gifts last a number of turns up to current level.

PackMentality.Title: Pack Mentality
PackMentality.Reminder: +1 To Hit if an ally is Nearby (add manually if so).

VipersAgility.Title: Viper's Agility
VipersAgility.Reminder: May make a second attack this round at disadvantage.

SlimySkin.Title: Slimy Skin
SlimySkin.Applied: Target ({name}) has Slimy Skin — -1 To Hit applied.

Regeneration.Title: Regeneration
Regeneration.Hint: Spend one round to restore 4+d4 HP.

ToxicSecretion.Title: Toxic Secretion
ToxicSecretion.Reminder: Took {damage} damage. If a melee attacker dealt it, they take 2 damage back.

Gadget.DropHintFilled: Drag more gadgets from the compendium to add them. Each gadget is drag-and-drop from the FLAIL Tinkerer Gadgets pack.
Gadget.DropHintEmpty: Drag Gadgets from the FLAIL Tinkerer Gadgets compendium onto this panel to acquire them.
Gadget.ReleaseHint: Release this gadget — rolls damage and posts the outcome to chat.
Gadget.ReleaseUsedHint: Already released today. Reset on long rest.

BuzzsawDisk.Label: d6 damage (ricochet on a 6)
BuzzsawDisk.TargetHint: Target the token first, then click Apply Damage.
BuzzsawDisk.RicochetDetail: Rolled a 6 — the disk ricochets onto another random target within Nearby range. GM picks the secondary target and applies the same damage.

ClockworkToy.Label: d8 delayed explosion (all Nearby)
ClockworkToy.TargetHint: Damage goes off next round to ALL targets Nearby. Target them before applying.
ClockworkToy.DelayedDetail: The toy walks erratically this round and explodes at the start of the next round, dealing the rolled damage to every target Nearby.

FireSpitter.Label: d4 flame cone (up to 2 Nearby targets)
FireSpitter.TargetHint: Target up to two Nearby tokens, then click Apply Damage — each takes the rolled amount.

GooBlast.Label: d8 delayed goo (all Nearby, next round)
GooBlast.TargetHint: Attaches to the initial target. Next round, all targets Nearby take the rolled damage.
GooBlast.DelayedDetail: Goo latches onto the initial target. On its next round, the goo detonates and deals the rolled damage to everyone Nearby (including the carrier).

ShockBolas.Label: d4 electrical (stun on a 4)
ShockBolas.TargetHint: Target the token first, then click Apply Damage.
ShockBolas.StunnedDetail: Rolled a 4 — the target is stunned. GM applies the Stunned condition alongside the damage.

JOAT.DropHint: Drag a Cutthroat talent, a Tinkerer gadget, or a wizard spell from its compendium onto this panel. Each pick is used once per day.
JOAT.CastHint: Cast the spell — max dice equals your Bard level (miscasting rules apply).
JOAT.MarkHint: Mark this pick as used today.
JOAT.UnmarkHint: Unmark — restore this pick for today.

Shapeshift.Hint: Roll up to six d6s to transform into a beast. The kingdom with the most primal gifts determines the form. Duration = dice rolled (in rounds). Each 1 grants +1 To Hit; each 6 is a mutation risk resolved on revert.
Shapeshift.PreviewNone: Select at least one primal gift to enable Shapeshift.
Shapeshift.DicePrompt: How many d6s do you want to roll?
Shapeshift.DiceHint: Choose 1 to 6 dice. Duration in rounds = number rolled. Each 1 adds +1 To Hit while shifted. Each 6 becomes a mutation risk when reverting.
Shapeshift.MutationWarning: {sixes} mutation risk(s) will trigger a save on revert.
Shapeshift.MutationPrompt: You rolled {n} six(es) during transformation. Choose one attribute for each mutation save. On a failed save, that attribute permanently loses 1 point.
Shapeshift.MutationHint: Saves are rolled 1d20 vs the current attribute score (roll-under). Nat 1 always passes, nat 20 always fails.
Shapeshift.NoLosses: All mutation saves passed — no permanent loss.
Shapeshift.WeaponLocked: Locked while shapeshifted — use a beast attack instead.

Rest.JackOfTradesReset: Jack of All Trades slots refreshed

Short.Title: Short Rest
Short.Tooltip: One turn resting — restore d4 HP.

Long.Title: Long Rest
Long.Tooltip: One watch eating and sleeping — restore d4+4 HP, clear Drained / Exhausted, reset Cleric fumble range and Bard JoT slots.

Full.Title: Full Rest
Full.Tooltip: A week in a safe place — restore all HP and clear most long-term conditions.
Full.Confirm: Take a full rest? This represents a week of in-fiction downtime. All HP will be restored and Drained / Exhausted / Injured conditions will be cleared.
